prompt.system_prompt:
[Role]
Below are the original Java class (targeting a specific method), the existing JUnit 5 test code, and the failed compile/runtime/assertion error log. Your task is to analyze all errors (including compile-time, runtime exceptions, and assertion failures) and fix the test code so that it compiles and runs correctly.

[**Task**]
- Identify errors and provide a fixed version of the JUnit 5 test class that compiles and runs successfully.

[**Key Guidelines**]
- Read the provided error log carefully to determine the problem.
- Retain existing useful test logic where possible.
- Modify or replace only what is necessary to resolve the issue.
- Follow the JUnit 5 rules.
- Do not use mock objects or abstractions unless absolutely necessary.
- If Errorguides is provided:
    It indicates that previous fixes did not resolve the issue.
    Pay extra attention to completely eliminating compile/runtime errors.
    Strictly follow the hints in Errorguides.
    While still preserving original test logic, allow slightly broader modifications if needed to ensure success.

[**Expected Results**]
- Generate a fully compilable JUnit 5 test class that can run without errors.
- Include necessary import statements as needed. - Name the class `public class { class name }_{ method name }_Test' and make sure it targets the method of your choice.

[emphasis/request]
Focus on minimal modifications to fix compile or runtime issues. Preserve original test logic as much as possible.
Do not deviate from the flow of the initial scenario

[output format]
Return **one top-level JSON object** with exactly the following keys:
{
  "Test": "```java\n// complete JUnit 5 test class here\n```"
}

For ease of parsing, enclose this part in three backticks and mark it as "json".
Only one JSON file should be provided.
Never remove or omit existing scenario code that is not related to the error.
prompt.user_prompt:
[Input]
- Library Name → word-wrap
- Class Name → org.davidmoten.text.utils.WordWrap
- Target Method → wrap
- Method Infomation :

- Test Scenarios :
{ TEST_SCENARIOS }

[Requirement Summary]
1. **Information**
- Existing Unit Test Code: package org.davidmoten.text.utils;

\npackage org.davidmoten.text.utils;\n\nimport static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.assertEquals;\nimport static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.assertThrows;\n\nimport java.io.File;\nimport java.io.StringReader;\nimport java.io.StringWriter;\nimport java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;\n\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.DisplayName;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\n\npublic class WordWrap_wrap_Test {\n\n    @Test\n    @DisplayName(\"wrap(Writer) ignores carriage return characters (CR) in input\")\n    public void test_TC11() {\n        // carriage return should be skipped (CR-branch) and not produce a newline or space\n        StringReader r = new StringReader(\"a\\rb\");\n        StringWriter w = new StringWriter();\n        WordWrap.from(r, true).wrap(w);\n        assertEquals(\"ab\", w.toString());\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    @DisplayName(\"wrap(Writer) with breakWords=false does not hyphen-break a long word and outputs it whole\")\n    public void test_TC12() {\n        // maxWidth=2 triggers tooLong repeatedly but breakWords=false should never hyphenate, output original\n        String input = \"abcdef\";\n        StringWriter w = new StringWriter();\n        WordWrap.from(input)\n                .maxWidth(2)\n                .breakWords(false)\n                .wrap(w);\n        assertEquals(\"abcdef\", w.toString());\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    @DisplayName(\"wrap(Writer) treats punctuation not in extraWordChars as separator and resets previousWasPunctuation flag\")\n    public void test_TC13() {\n        // '*' is punctuation and not in extraWordChars, so it is treated as separator, then 'a' starts new word\n        String text = \"*a b\";\n        StringWriter w = new StringWriter();\n        WordWrap.from(text)\n                .maxWidth(10)\n                .wrap(w);\n        assertEquals(\"*a b\", w.toString());\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    @DisplayName(\"from(File,Charset) throws IORuntimeException when input file does not exist\")\n    public void test_TC14() {\n        // nonexistent file should cause FileNotFoundException and be wrapped into IORuntimeException\n        File f = new File(\"nonexistent12345.txt\");\n        assertThrows(IORuntimeException.class, () -> WordWrap.from(f, StandardCharsets.UTF_8));\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    @DisplayName(\"wrap(Writer) outputs custom newLine string CRLF when newLine is set to \" +\n                   "\"\\r\\n\"\")\n    public void test_TC15() {\n        // space between 'x' and 'y' triggers line break at maxWidth=1, newLine CRLF used\n        String text = \"x y\";\n        StringWriter w = new StringWriter();\n        WordWrap.from(text)\n                .maxWidth(1)\n                .newLine(\"\\r\\n\")\n                .wrap(w);\n        assertEquals(\"x\\r\\ny\", w.toString());\n    }\n}\n

- Analyze error messages to determine the root cause and location of the error.
- Modify the test code so that it compiles and runs successfully.
- Do not change the number of scenarios.
- Avoid unnecessary mocks or abstractions.
- Do not make any changes that are not related to the error message.
- Do NOT summarize or skip scenario.
- Commented-out tests indicate test cases that experienced runtime errors or compilation errors.
- Please ensure that no infinite loops occur.

3. **End Results**
- A single JUnit 5 test class that compiles and runs.
- Add useful comments or use @DisplayName to clearly explain what has been changed or added.
- Briefly summarize what has been changed and why.

4. **Completeness**
- Create a fully compilable JUnit 5 test class that can run without errors.
- Include any necessary imports and reflection helpers as needed.
- Name the class `public class org.davidmoten.text.utils.WordWrap_{ METHOD_NAME }_Test' and ensure that it targets the method of your choice.
